title: hieracium tephrosoma subsp. amaurocranum
intro: Hieracium tephrosoma subsp. amaurocranum, a rare and lesser-known species of the Hawkweed genus, is a flowering plant characterized by its small, yellow flowers and narrow leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, primarily due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Hieracium tephrosoma subsp. amaurocranum include potential uses as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent, as well as a natural remedy for wound healing and skin issues. In herbal preparations, the plant can be used as a tincture, tea, or topical cream, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions.
